methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.5B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Helion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series F), post-money $5.4B

data.narrative:
Climeworks, a Series F climate and energy technology company that has raised over $1.0B to date, is evaluated against a peer universe of 9 private firms and 1 public comparable (Lanzatech, LNZA) operating in the same sector and stage. Key private peers include Helion, TAE Technologies, Form Energy, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and Redwood Materials.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $1.0B total raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 1.3x multiplier typical for Series F advancement, yielding $1.3B. Peer-median methodology draws on three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Helion at $5.4B (Series F), TAE Technologies at $6.0B (Series G), and Form Energy at $3.5B (Series G). The median valuation across these three peers is $5.4B, with a range of $3.5B to $6.0B.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $1.1B to $6.2B, with a median of $3.4B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around the appropriate peer weighting and stage-specific step-up rates in a capital-intensive, pre-revenue-dominant sector. Stage-progression alone produces a conservative floor; peer comparables suggest substantially higher valuations typical for later-stage climate-tech funding rounds. Key variables driving valuation within this range include deployment momentum, capital efficiency relative to peers, and path to commercialization timing.
